title: AIContext
description: AI Context (also called a Context Profile) for a data asset. A normalized, LLM-ready projection of an asset's structural signals and the business knowledge attached to it (glossary terms, metrics, Context Center articles). It is assembled server-side so any agent can pull the full context for an asset of any type (table, dashboard, pipeline, topic, ...) in a single call to give strong signals to downstream tasks such as SQL generation. The common envelope (identity, knowledge, lineage) applies to every entity type; the type-specific structural details live under `assetContext`.

knowledgeItem.description: A single piece of business knowledge attached to the asset (glossary term definition, metric definition, or Context Center article/pill).

id.description: Unique identifier of the knowledge entity.

type.description: The entity type of the knowledge item.

content.description: The definition, business rule, metric expression, or article body for this item. May be a bounded lead excerpt when contentTruncated is true, or null when omitted to stay within the context budget.

contentTruncated.description: True when content is a lead excerpt or has been omitted to fit the context budget; fetch the full body via the get_knowledge_content tool using this item's fullyQualifiedName.

fieldContext.description: Compact, query-relevant description of a field. Reused across the columnar/field-bearing asset types (table columns, topic schema fields, search-index fields, ML features, container columns, API request/response fields).

dataTypeEnum.description: Raw data-type enum name of the field (e.g. VARCHAR, BIGINT — a ColumnDataType value for table columns), for programmatic type branching. `dataType` may carry the source display form (e.g. varchar(255)).

constraint.description: Field-level constraint (e.g. PRIMARY_KEY, NOT_NULL), when applicable.

foreignKey.description: A foreign-key relationship derived from a table's constraints. Provides explicit join keys to other tables.

columns.description: Local column names participating in the foreign key.

referredColumns.description: Fully qualified names of the referenced columns in the parent table(s).

joinHint.description: An empirical join between a column of this table and a column of another table, derived from observed query usage. A strong, data-driven signal for how tables are joined in practice.

column.description: Local column name on this table.

joinedWith.description: Fully qualified name of the column this column is frequently joined with.

joinCount.description: Number of observed joins over the recorded window.

tableContext.description: Table-specific structural context (schema, keys, joins) that gives strong signals for SQL generation.

primaryKey.description: Column names forming the primary key.

partitionColumns.description: Column names the table is partitioned on.

schemaDefinition.description: DDL for tables and views, when available.

dataModel.description: The dbt/DDL model that produces this table (type, path, and defining SQL), when available.

tableDataModel.description: The data model that produces a table — the dbt (or DDL) model behind it: its type, where the model file lives, and the SQL that defines the table. A strong signal for how the table is built and what logic it encodes.

modelType.description: Model type that produced the table (e.g. DBT or DDL).

path.description: Path to the model definition file (e.g. the dbt `.sql` model path).

sourceProject.description: The dbt project the model belongs to, when applicable.

sql.description: SQL that defines the table: the compiled dbt/DDL model SQL when available, otherwise the raw templated model SQL. May be bounded server-side.

dashboardContext.description: Dashboard-specific context: the charts it renders, its data models, and the tables it is ultimately backed by.

charts.description: Fully qualified names of the charts on this dashboard.

dataModels.description: Fully qualified names of the data models backing this dashboard.

sourceTables.description: Fully qualified names of upstream tables that feed this dashboard.

pipelineContext.description: Pipeline-specific context: its tasks and the assets it reads from and writes to.

sourceAssets.description: Fully qualified names of assets this pipeline reads from.

sinkAssets.description: Fully qualified names of assets this pipeline writes to.

topicContext.description: Topic-specific context: its message schema and partitioning.

genericAssetContext.description: Fallback structural context for asset types without a dedicated context (container, mlmodel, searchIndex, storedProcedure, apiEndpoint, ...). Holds the asset's fields/columns and an optional definition (DDL, code, or query).

definition.description: DDL, stored-procedure code, or query text backing this asset, when available.

sourceAssets.description: Fully qualified names of upstream assets, when known.

columnProfileSummary.description: Latest profiled shape of a column: the signals an LLM needs to write accurate filters (null ratio, cardinality, observed value bounds).

nullProportion.description: Fraction of rows where this column is null (0..1).

uniqueProportion.description: Fraction of rows whose value occurs exactly once (0..1) — near 1 suggests a key/identifier column.

mean.description: Mean value (numeric/date columns; the profiler stores string-length stats here for text columns).

median.description: Median value (numeric/date columns; the profiler stores string-length stats here for text columns).

cardinalityDistribution.description: Top observed values with counts and percentages (plus an 'Others' bucket), when the profiler computed it — lets an agent write exact filter predicates.

dataQuality.description: Data-quality standing of the asset, so an agent can caveat its answer when tests are failing or an incident is open.

openIncidents.description: Number of currently-open (unresolved) data-quality incidents on the asset.

observability.description: Runtime signals about the asset: its profiled shape (for constructing accurate queries) and its data-quality/incident standing (for qualifying answers). Volatile — assembled on read, not part of the semantic embedding.

profiledAt.description: Timestamp of the latest profile run.

profileSample.description: Sample size the profile ran on — a percentage when profileSampleType is PERCENTAGE, otherwise an absolute row count. Absent when the profile ran on the full dataset.

profileSampleType.description: Interpretation of profileSample: PERCENTAGE or ROWS.

assetContext.description: Type-specific structural context. Exactly one sub-context is populated, selected by the asset's entity type. New asset types are added as new optional sub-contexts here without breaking existing consumers.

lineageEdgeContext.description: One immediate lineage edge of the asset: the neighboring asset's fully qualified name plus the column-level mappings recorded on the edge, when available.

fullyQualifiedName.description: Fully qualified name of the neighboring upstream or downstream asset.

columns.description: Column-level lineage recorded on this edge, capped server-side; fetch the full graph with the get_entity_lineage tool.

columnsTruncated.description: True when column-level lineage was truncated at the server-side per-edge cap; fetch the full graph with the get_entity_lineage tool.

id.description: Unique identifier of the asset this context describes.

name.description: Name of the asset this context describes.

fullyQualifiedName.description: Fully qualified name of the asset this context describes.

entityType.description: Entity type of the asset (table, dashboard, pipeline, topic, ...).

service.description: Reference (id, name, type) to the service the asset belongs to, when applicable.

serviceType.description: Service type of the asset's service (e.g. Snowflake, BigQuery).

resource.description: Canonical URI of the asset this context describes (the OKF `resource` frontmatter key).

tags.description: Classification tags and tier applied to the asset (tag fully qualified names).

glossaryTerms.description: Approved glossary terms attached to the asset (asset-level and field/column-level).

articles.description: Context Center articles and knowledge pills attached to the asset.

upstreamEdges.description: Immediate upstream lineage edges with optional column mappings. Supplements the upstream fully qualified name list.

downstreamEdges.description: Immediate downstream lineage edges with optional column mappings. Supplements the downstream fully qualified name list.

assetContext.description: Type-specific structural context for the asset.

observability.description: Runtime signals (profiled shape + data-quality standing) for query construction and answer qualification.

fingerprint.description: Content fingerprint of this context, including attached knowledge, used to detect staleness.

generatedAt.description: Time at which this context was assembled.
